rows.name: Desc_1
rows.content:
Welcome to Barrow Brown Carrington

Thank you for choosing us to represent you in your divorce. Client care and satisfaction are our priority. Please don't hesitate to contact our office with any questions or concerns throughout your divorce.

The following questionnaire is designed to collect all of the information required by the court in a divorce. Your information is kept in the strictest of confidence and our questionnaire is equipped with bank-grade security to protect you.

For your convenience, your progress is automatically saved at the end of each page. But, you can save your progress manually by clicking the "Save Progress" button at the bottom of the page. As you complete the questionnaire, you may find that you need to step away to find information that is not readily available.

Again, we thank you for choosing Barrow Brown Carrington. We are thrilled to be your attorneys.


With best regards,

Hugh, Charlie, and Brendon

rows.name: AYC_Desc
rows.content: Below, you are asked to select the state and county in which you will be filing. You should select the state and county in which either you or your spouse currently reside.

rows.name: FilingState
rows.label: In which state will you be filing?

rows.name: FilingCounty
rows.label: In which county in this state will you be filing?

rows.name: AYCC_Desc

rows.name: KRS_Heading
rows.content: Kentucky Revised Statute (KRS) 403.170: No decree shall be entered until the parties have lived apart for 60 days. Living apart shall include living under the same roof without sexual cohabitation.

rows.name: SeparationDate
rows.label: Date of Separation

rows.name: PetPregnant_Heading
rows.content: If you are pregnant with a child of the marriage you can still file for divorce. The Court cannot, however, finalize your divorce until you give birth. If you are pregnant with a child of which your spouse is not a parent, it will not affect your divorce.

rows.name: RespPregnant_Heading
rows.content: If your spouse is pregnant with a child of the marriage you can still file for divorce. The Court cannot, however, finalize your divorce until they give birth. If they are pregnant with a child of which you are not a parent, it will not affect your divorce.

rows.name: KRS_Insurance_Info
rows.content: Kentucky Revised Statute (KRS) 403.211 allows the court to allocate the cost of medical, dental and/or vision insurance FOR THE CHILD(REN) between the parties. If the child is covered through your employer's family medical coverage plan, you can determine the cost of the child only by subtracting the cost of the plan for you alone from the cost of the plan that includes you and the child(ren).

rows.name: ChildInsuranceCost
rows.label: What is the MONTHLY cost for medical, dental, and/or vision insurance FOR THE {{$if(length(ChildInfo) == 1, 'CHILD', 'CHILDREN')}} ONLY?

rows.name: CACS_Heading
rows.content:
Custody and Child Support

Generally, custody determines a parents right to participate in decision making regarding their child. These decisions may include educational decisions, medical decision, or religious decisions. Custody is only taken from a parent by the court if there is sufficient evidence to prove that parent is so grossly irresponsible that they can no longer safely participate in decision making regarding their child. The division of parenting time is a separate issue from custody, and it is also addressed below.

rows.name: Custody_Video
rows.content: <youtube id='mHS6YKyCleM' style='{"display":"table","margin":"auto"}' title='Barrow Brown Carrington Explains Child Custody'/>

rows.name: JointCustody
rows.label: Will you and your spouse have joint custody of your {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}}?

rows.name: SoleCustodian
rows.label: Which party will have custody of your {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}}?

rows.name: ParentingSchedule_Video
rows.content: <youtube id='v_tRhqZ9Bo0' style='{"display":"table","margin":"auto"}' title='Barrow Brown Carrington Explains a Parenting Schedule'/>

rows.name: ParentingScheduleYesNo
rows.label: Does your agreement need to include a detailed parenting time schedule?

rows.name: CACS_Heading
rows.content: You will be prompted to complete a separate parenting time questionnaire upon completion of this questionnaire.

rows.name: ParentingSchedule
rows.label: Please select from one of the following parenting time arrangements:
options: {{PetFirst}} has the {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}} primarily, and {{RespFirst}} has time by agreement.
{{RespFirst}} has the {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}} primarily, and {{PetFirst}} has time by agreement.

rows.name: PetOvernights
rows.label: How many overnights with the {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}} will you have annually?
rows.tooltip: The court defines 181-183 annual overnights to be 50%. If a parent will only have overnights on the weekend, this will typically mean 96-100 overnights.

rows.name: RespOvernights
rows.label: How many overnights with the {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}} will your spouse have annually?
rows.tooltip: The court defines 181-183 annual overnights to be 50%. If a parent will only have overnights on the weekend, this will typically mean 96-100 overnights.

rows.name: ControlledExpenses_DESC
rows.content:
When both parents equally share parenting time, the court must determine which parent will pay the controlled expenses. This type of expense for the {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}} is typically paid by the custodial parent and is not transferred or duplicated.
Controlled expenses are items like clothing, education, school books and supplies, ordinary uninsured health care and personal care. “Education” expenses include ordinary costs assessed to all students, such as textbook rental, laboratory fees, and lunches, which should be paid by the custodial parent. The cost of participating in elective school activities such as sports, performing arts and clubs, as well as related extracurricular activities are “optional” activities are not included.
rules: ParentingSchedule == 'Equally divide time between parties.' or (PetOvernights > 180 and RespOvernights > 180)

rows.name: ControlledExpensesPayer
rows.label: Who will pay the controlled expenses?
rules: ParentingSchedule == 'Equally divide time between parties.' or (PetOvernights > 180 and RespOvernights > 180)

rows.name: ChildSupport
rows.label: Will either you or your spouse be paying child support?

rows.name: ChildSupportPayer
rows.label: Who will be paying child support?

rows.name: PetChildSupport
rows.label: What is the monthly amount that you have agreed to pay to your spouse as child support?

rows.name: RespChildSupport
rows.label: What is the monthly amount the your spouse has agreed to pay to you as child support?

rows.name: ChildTaxClaimant
rows.label: Which party will be claiming the {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}} on their taxes?

options.label:
We will split them until one child remains then alternate.
For odd numbers of children - split evenly and alternate one child
options.value:
We will split them until one child remains then alternate.
For odd numbers of children - split evenly and alternate one child

rows.name: ChildTaxInitParty
rows.label: Which party will claim the {{$if(length(ChildInfo) > 1, 'children', 'child')}} first?
rules: ChildTaxClaimant == 'We will alternate claiming the child' or ChildTaxClaimant == 'We will alternate claiming the children'

rows.name: AYP_Heading
rows.content:
About Your Property

The remainder of this questionnaire requests information about your property and your spouse's property. The court requires parties to divorce to make complete disclosure of all of their assets and debts to one another.

As you complete this questionnaire, please respond as to your property. If you know about your spouse's property, please include that information as well. If you do not know about your spouse's property, simply include information about your property.

Please remember, your spouse is required to make full and complete disclosure of all assets, debts, and income. If your spouse is unwilling to disclose such information, and you would like to pursue your right to full and complete disclosure, please contact our office. Our attorneys can help you get the information that you are entitled to. However, this service is not included in our flat-fee uncontested divorce, and it is an indication that your divorce may not be uncontested.

rows.name: Custody_Video
rows.content: <youtube id='5HJpT8QTpBw' style='{"display":"table","margin":"auto"}' title='Barrow Brown Carrington Family Attorneys Explain Property Division'/>

rows.name: RealEstateYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse own real estate?

rows.name: REConfirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose all real estate property you own. If a real estate is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the real estate. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including real estate properties.

rows.name: NoRealEstateConfirmation
rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse own any real estate property? This includes any homes, property being rented out, empty land, etc.

Vehicles

(Cars, Boats, RVs, Trailers, ATVs, etc.)

rows.name: Vehicles_Confirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose all vehicles you own. If a vehicle is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the vehicle. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including vehicles.

rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse own any vehicles? This includes any cars, trucks, boats, trailers, ATVs, etc.

rows.name: Banks_Heading
rows.content:
Bank Account Information

Please list all bank accounts in your and your spouse's names. These may include checking, savings, and brokerage accounts. Joint accounts must be assigned to one party. Any needed equalization of the division of these accounts will be addressed later in this questionnaire.

rows.name: OwnBankAccountsYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse own any bank accounts?

rows.name: Banks_Confirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose all bank accounts you own. If an account is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the account. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including bank accounts.

rows.name: NoBanksAccountsOwnedConfirmation
rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse own any bank accounts? This includes checking, savings, and brokerage accounts owned in your name alone or jointly.

rows.name: BankInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each bank account owned by you or your spouse:

rows.label: Name of bank:

rows.label: Account Type:

rows.name: BankInfo.{{$index}}.Balance
rows.label: Current account balance:

rows.name: BankInfo.{{$index}}.Owner
rows.label: Account owner:

rows.name: BankInfo.{{$index}}.Keeper
rows.label: Which party will retain this account:

rows.name: Retirement_Heading
rows.content:
Retirement Benefits

Please provide information about any retirement accounts that you or your spouse own. These accounts may include IRA, Keogh, 401(k), 403(b), Pension, Thrift Savings Account, and all other retirement accounts.

rows.name: RetirementAccountOwnedYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse own a retirement account?

rows.name: Retirement_Confirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose all retirement accounts you own. If an account is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the account. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including retirement accounts.

rows.name: NoRetirementAccountOwnedConfirmation
rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse own any retirement accounts? This includes any IRA, Keogh, 401(k), 403(b), Pension, Thrift Savings Account, etc. It does not include any investment accounts. Those accounts will be covered later in the form.

rows.name: RetireInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each retirement account owned by you or your spouse:

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.Name
rows.label: Financial Institution:
rows.tooltip: Most plans are administered by institutions like Fidelity, Vanguard, Charles Schwab, etc.

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.Type
rows.label: Account Type:
rows.tooltip: The account type may be 401(k), IRA, 403(b), etc.

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.Owner
rows.label: Account owner:

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.Balance
rows.label: Account Balance:

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.Loan
rows.label: Loan Balance:
rows.tooltip: If there is a loan secured by the retirement account, please enter the balance of the loan here.

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.Vested
rows.label: Is this account fully or partially vested?
rows.tooltip: A vesting schedule is the term of employment an employee must reach in order to receive certain benefits, such as retirement benefits, stock options, etc.

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.Divide
rows.label: Will this account be divided?

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.PetDisposition
rows.label: How will this account be divided?
options: 1/2 of Marital Portion of Account to {{PetFirst}}
rows.tooltip: The marital portion of a retirement account is the amount of contributions to the account during the marriage, plus or minus any market fluctuation on that amount.

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.RespDisposition
rows.label: How will this account be divided?
options: 1/2 of Marital Portion of Account to {{RespFirst}}
rows.tooltip: The marital portion of a retirement account is the amount of contributions to the account during the marriage, plus or minus any market fluctuation on that amount.

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.PetTransfer
rows.label: What amount will be transferred to {{PetFirst}}

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.RespTransfer
rows.label: What amount will be transferred to {{RespFirst}}

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.RespTransferPercent
rows.label: What percentage will be transferred to {{RespFirst}}

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.ValuationDate
rows.label: When will be the valuation date?

rows.name: RetireInfo.{{$index}}.ValuationDateOther
rows.label: Date of valuation

rows.name: Investment_Heading
rows.content:
Investment Accounts

This section seeks information regarding investment accounts that you or your spouse own. These accounts may include stock, certificates of deposit, mutual funds, bonds, portfolios, or any other investment or retirement account. Any needed equalization of the division of these accounts will be addressed later in this questionnaire.

rows.name: InvestmentOwnedYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse own any investment accounts?

rows.name: Investment_Confirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose all investment accounts you own. If an account is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the account. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including investment accounts.

rows.name: NoInvestmentOwnedConfirmation
rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse own any investment accounts? This includes any stock, certificates of deposit, mutual funds, bonds, portfolios, etc.

rows.name: InvestmentInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each investment account owned by you or your spouse:

rows.name: InvestmentInfo.{{$index}}.Name
rows.label: Financial Institution:
rows.tooltip: Examples may included Merrill Lynch, Hilliard Lyons, Charles Schwab, Vanguard, Ameriprise, and others.

rows.name: InvestmentInfo.{{$index}}.Balance
rows.label: Current Account Value:

rows.name: InvestmentInfo.{{$index}}.Owner
rows.label: Account owner:

rows.name: InvestmentInfo.{{$index}}.Type
rows.label: Account Type

rows.name: InvestmentInfo.{{$index}}.Disposition
rows.label: Which party will retain this account?

rows.name: LifeInsOwnedYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse own any life insurance policies?

rows.name: Insurance_Confirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose all life insurance policies you own. If a policy is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the policy. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including life insurance policies.

rows.name: NoLifeInsOwnedConfirmation
rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse own any life insurance policies?

rows.name: LifeInsuranceInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each life insurance policy owned by you or your spouse:

rows.name: LifeInsuranceInfo.{{$index}}.Company
rows.label: Insurance Company:

rows.name: LifeInsuranceInfo.{{$index}}.Insured
rows.label: Whose life is insured?

rows.name: LifeInsuranceInfo.{{$index}}.Beneficiary
rows.label: Beneficiary:

rows.name: LifeInsuranceInfo.{{$index}}.CashPolicy
rows.label: What is the cash surrender value? (Enter 0 if this policy has no cash surrender value)
rows.tooltip: Whole life insurance policies typically have a cash surrender value that increases as you pay the premiums. Term life policies, including most employer-provided life insurance policies, do not have a cash surrender value.

rows.name: LifeInsuranceInfo.{{$index}}.Owner
rows.label: Policy Owner:
rows.tooltip: If you jointly own the life insurance policy with a third party (someone other than your spouse), please select your name as the owner.

rows.name: LifeInsuranceInfo.{{$index}}.Disposition
rows.label: Which party will retain this policy?

rows.name: Business_Heading
rows.content:
Business Ownerships or Interests

If either you or your spouse own a business, or an interest in a business, please list the information requested about the business below. Any needed equalization of the division of these business interests will be addressed later in this questionnaire.

rows.name: BusinessOwnedYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse own a business?

rows.name: BusinessInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each business owned by you or your spouse:

rows.name: BusinessInfo.{{$index}}.Name
rows.label: Business Name:

rows.name: BusinessInfo.{{$index}}.Owner
rows.label: Business Owner:
rows.tooltip: If you do not own 100% of the business, please respond to this question regarding the portion of the business that you or your spouse own only. If the business is partially owned by a third party (someone other than you or your spouse), their interest in the business will not be affected by this divorce. We only need information about your and/or your spouse's interest in the business, whether it be 1% or 100% of the business.

rows.name: BusinessInfo.{{$index}}.Percentage
rows.label: What percentage of this business do you or your spouse own?

rows.name: BusinessInfo.{{$index}}.Value
rows.label: Value of business interest:
rows.tooltip: Please list the value of your or your spouse's interest in the business. If your interest in the business is less than 100%, then the value entered here should be less than the value of the entire business. Leave blank if the value is unknown.

rows.name: BusinessInfo.{{$index}}.Disposition
rows.label: Which party will retain this business?

rows.name: Assets_Heading
rows.content:
Other Assets

Other assets may include large cash holdings, precious metals, promissory notes, copyrights, trademarks, pets or animals, jewelry, collectibles, tools, inventions, or other assets not previously listed.

rows.name: OtherAssetsYesNo
rows.label: Are there any other assets that you or your spouse have agreed to specifically address?

rows.name: Assets_Confirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose any other assets you own. If an asset is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the asset. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including any other assets not yet covered.

rows.name: NoOtherAssetsConfirmation
rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse own any other assets that need division? Such assets can include large cash holdings, precious metals, promissory notes, copyrights, trademarks, pets or animals, jewelry, collectibles, tools, inventions, etc. This does not include debts, those will be covered in a later section.

rows.name: OtherAssetInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each of the additional assets to specifically address:

rows.name: OtherAssetInfo.{{$index}}.Name
rows.label: Brief Description:

rows.name: OtherAssetInfo.{{$index}}.Value
rows.label: Asset value:

rows.name: OtherAssetInfo.{{$index}}.Disposition
rows.label: Which party will retain this asset?

rows.name: Debt_Heading
rows.content:
Debt

Other debt not previously listed may include credit card debt, credit union loans, student loans, and other unsecured debt.

rows.name: OtherDebtsYesNo
rows.label: Do you or your spouse have any debts not previously listed?

rows.name: Debts_Confirm_Heading
rows.content: It is critical that you disclose all debts you own. If a debt is undisclosed, it will not be addressed in the divorce documents. This means that your spouse could file a claim with the court in the future seeking an award from the debt. He or she may also seek sanctions. To ensure finality in the division of property, we must disclose all property you own in your name alone or jointly, including debts.

rows.name: NoOtherDebtsConfirmation
rows.label: Are you sure neither you nor your spouse any other debts not yet listed? Such debts can include credit card debt, credit union loans, student loans, etc.

rows.name: DebtInfo
rows.label: Please enter the following information about each debt that has not yet been listed:

rows.name: DebtInfo.{{$index}}.Creditor
rows.label: Name of Creditor:

rows.name: DebtInfo.{{$index}}.Balance
rows.label: Balance of Debt:

rows.name: DebtInfo.{{$index}}.Party
rows.label: Party named on debt:

rows.name: DebtInfo.{{$index}}.Disposition
rows.label: Which party will take responsibility for this debt?

rows.name: Equalization_Heading
rows.content:
Equalization Payment

If one spouse or the other is receiving a greater portion of assets or debts in a settlement agreement, the imbalance may be corrected by an equalization payment. The payment is a tax-free transfer from one spouse to the other in divorce. This payment is not required, and if there is no imbalance in the division of assets and debts, the payment is inappropriate.

rows.name: Equalization_Video
rows.content: <youtube id='YPWGgikvTWY' style='{"display":"table","margin":"auto"}' title='Barrow Brown Carrington Explains an Equalization Payment'/>

rows.name: RespEqualizationPaymentYesNo
rows.label: Will you receive any payment from your spouse to equalize the division of assets and debts in your agreement?
rows.tooltip: If one spouse or the other is receiving a greater portion of assets or debts in a settlement agreement, the imbalance may be corrected by an equalization payment. The payment is a one-time, tax-free transfer from one spouse to the other in divorce. This payment is not required, and if there is no imbalance in the division of assets and debts, the payment is inappropriate.

rows.name: PetEqualizationPaymentYesNo
rows.label: Will your spouse receive any payment from you to equalize the division of assets and debts in your agreement?
rows.tooltip: If one spouse or the other is receiving a greater portion of assets or debts in a settlement agreement, the imbalance may be corrected by an equalization payment. The payment is a one-time, tax-free transfer from one spouse to the other in divorce. This payment is not required, and if there is no imbalance in the division of assets and debts, the payment is inappropriate.

rows.name: RespEqualizationAmount
rows.label: What amount will your spouse pay to you as an equalization payment?

rows.name: PetEqualizationAmount
rows.label: What amount will you pay to your spouse as an equalization payment?

rows.name: EqualizationType
rows.label: Please select how the equalization payment will be paid:

rows.name: EqualizationMonthlyPayment
rows.label: Please list the agreed upon monthly payment amount:

rows.name: Maintenance_Heading
rows.content:
Maintenance

Maintenance is spousal support. Maintenance payments are typically paid monthly by one spouse to their former spouse for a period of months. When there is a large disparity in the parties' income, maintenance may be a consideration for the parties.

rows.name: Maintenance
rows.label: Will you or your spouse be paying maintenance to the other?

rows.name: MaintenancePayer
rows.label: Who will be paying maintenance?

rows.name: MaintenanceType
rows.label: What type of maintenance will be paid?

rows.name: MaintenanceTerm
rows.label: How many months will maintenance be paid?

rows.name: MaintenanceTermRehabilitative
rows.label: How many months will maintenance be paid?
rows.tooltip: Rehabilitative maintenance cannot have a term longer than 36 months (3 years).

rows.name: MaintenanceSum
rows.label: What is the MONTHLY amount of maintenance to be paid?

rows.name: AdditionalNotes
rows.label: Please specify any additional notes, terms, or items that you would like added to the agreement:
